task_description: Compare Paris, Rome, and Barcelona as vacation destinations for a 7-day trip

attributes.name: description
attributes.value: The City of Light captivates with its iconic landmarks, world-class museums, charming cafés, and romantic ambiance. From the Eiffel Tower to the Louvre, Paris offers unparalleled art, culture, and cuisine.

attributes.name: overall_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Paris ranks exceptionally high due to its unmatched cultural offerings, iconic landmarks like the Eiffel Tower and Louvre, world-renowned cuisine, and romantic atmosphere. The city excels in art, architecture, and gastronomy, though it can be expensive and crowded during peak season.

attributes.name: culture_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Paris is home to the Louvre, Musée d'Orsay, Centre Pompidou, and countless other world-class museums. The city's architecture spans from medieval Notre-Dame to modern La Défense. Rich literary, artistic, and philosophical heritage permeates every arrondissement.

attributes.name: food_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Paris is the global capital of gastronomy with the most Michelin-starred restaurants in the world. From croissants and baguettes to haute cuisine, the city offers exceptional dining at every price point. Markets, bistros, and patisseries showcase France's culinary excellence.

attributes.name: safety_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Paris is generally safe for tourists with good police presence and emergency services. Petty crime like pickpocketing is common in tourist areas and metro stations. Certain neighborhoods should be avoided at night. Overall, standard urban precautions are sufficient.

attributes.name: walkability_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Paris is exceptionally walkable with wide boulevards, pedestrian zones, and compact arrondissements. Many major attractions are within walking distance. The metro system is excellent for longer distances. Beautiful architecture makes walking a pleasure.

attributes.name: estimated_daily_cost
attributes.help_text: Average daily cost per person in EUR

attributes.name: total_estimated_cost_7days
attributes.help_text: Total estimated cost for 7 days (excluding flights)

value.name: Eiffel Tower
value.description: Iconic iron lattice tower built in 1889, offering panoramic views of Paris from its observation decks

value.name: Louvre Museum
value.description: World's largest art museum housing the Mona Lisa, Venus de Milo, and over 380,000 works of art

value.name: Notre-Dame Cathedral
value.description: Medieval Gothic cathedral on Île de la Cité, currently under restoration after 2019 fire

value.name: Arc de Triomphe
value.description: Monumental arch honoring those who fought for France, center of the Place Charles de Gaulle

value.name: Sacré-Cœur Basilica
value.description: White-domed basilica atop Montmartre hill with stunning city views and artistic neighborhood

value.name: Musée d'Orsay
value.description: Impressionist and Post-Impressionist masterpieces in a converted Beaux-Arts railway station

value.name: Champs-Élysées
value.description: Famous avenue lined with luxury shops, cafés, and theaters leading to the Arc de Triomphe

value.name: Versailles Palace
value.description: Opulent former royal residence with Hall of Mirrors and extensive formal gardens (30 min from Paris)

attributes.name: description
attributes.value: The Eternal City is a living museum where ancient ruins stand alongside Renaissance masterpieces. Rome offers unparalleled history, from the Colosseum to the Vatican, combined with exceptional Italian cuisine and vibrant street life.

attributes.name: overall_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Rome excels in historical significance with unmatched ancient ruins and Renaissance art. The Vatican Museums, Colosseum, and Roman Forum are bucket-list attractions. Authentic Italian cuisine and charming piazzas add to the appeal. Can be crowded and chaotic, with occasional service issues.

attributes.name: culture_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Rome's 2,800-year history is visible everywhere. The Colosseum, Roman Forum, and Pantheon showcase ancient engineering. The Vatican houses Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel and St. Peter's Basilica. Baroque fountains, Renaissance palaces, and archaeological treasures make this an unparalleled cultural destination.

attributes.name: food_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Authentic Roman cuisine features pasta classics like carbonara, amatriciana, and cacio e pepe. Thin-crust pizza, supplì, and gelato are exceptional. Traditional trattorias serve simple, high-quality dishes. Food is generally more affordable than Paris while maintaining excellent quality.

attributes.name: safety_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Rome is reasonably safe with major tourist areas well-patrolled. Pickpocketing is common near attractions like the Trevi Fountain and on crowded buses. Scams targeting tourists are frequent. Some neighborhoods should be avoided at night. Traffic can be chaotic and aggressive.

attributes.name: walkability_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Rome's historic center is compact and walkable with many attractions within reasonable distance. Cobblestone streets and seven hills can be challenging. The metro is limited but useful. Walking between ruins, fountains, and piazzas is the best way to experience the city.

attributes.name: estimated_daily_cost
attributes.help_text: Average daily cost per person in EUR

attributes.name: total_estimated_cost_7days
attributes.help_text: Total estimated cost for 7 days (excluding flights)

value.name: Colosseum
value.description: Ancient amphitheater built in 80 AD, iconic symbol of Imperial Rome where gladiators fought

value.name: Vatican Museums & Sistine Chapel
value.description: Vast collection of art and historical treasures culminating in Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel ceiling

value.name: St. Peter's Basilica
value.description: Renaissance church and largest Christian basilica, with Michelangelo's dome and Pietà sculpture

value.name: Roman Forum
value.description: Ancient civic center with ruins of temples, basilicas, and government buildings from Roman Empire

value.name: Trevi Fountain
value.description: Baroque masterpiece and largest fountain in Rome, famous for coin-tossing tradition

value.name: Pantheon
value.description: Best-preserved ancient Roman building with massive dome and oculus, now a church

value.name: Spanish Steps
value.description: Monumental 135-step stairway connecting Piazza di Spagna with Trinità dei Monti church

value.name: Piazza Navona
value.description: Baroque square built on ancient stadium with Bernini's Fountain of the Four Rivers

attributes.name: description
attributes.value: A vibrant Mediterranean city blending Gaudí's whimsical modernist architecture with beautiful beaches, Gothic quarters, and lively street culture. Barcelona offers art, beaches, nightlife, and exceptional Catalan cuisine in a cosmopolitan setting.

attributes.name: overall_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Barcelona uniquely combines urban culture with beach relaxation. Gaudí's Sagrada Familia and Park Güell are architectural masterpieces. The Gothic Quarter offers medieval charm while Barceloneta provides beach access. Vibrant nightlife, excellent tapas, and a more laid-back atmosphere than Paris or Rome make it highly appealing.

attributes.name: culture_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Barcelona showcases Catalan modernism through Gaudí's unfinished Sagrada Familia and whimsical Park Güell. The Gothic Quarter preserves medieval history. Picasso Museum and Joan Miró Foundation highlight artistic heritage. Strong regional identity, language, and traditions distinguish Barcelona from the rest of Spain.

attributes.name: food_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Catalan cuisine offers exceptional tapas, fresh seafood, and innovative cooking. La Boqueria market showcases regional ingredients. Pintxos bars and vermouth culture are highlights. More affordable than Paris with generous portions. Paella, jamón ibérico, and cava wine are must-tries.

attributes.name: safety_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Barcelona is generally safe with good emergency services. Pickpocketing is very common in tourist areas, especially Las Ramblas and metro. Bag snatching from outdoor cafés occurs. Some neighborhoods like El Raval require caution at night. Overall, standard vigilance is needed but violent crime is rare.

attributes.name: walkability_rating_reasoning
attributes.value: Barcelona's grid layout and wide boulevards make walking easy. Most attractions are concentrated in Ciutat Vella and Eixample districts. The beachfront promenade is perfect for strolling. Excellent metro and bus systems complement walking. Bike lanes and rental systems add to mobility options.

attributes.name: estimated_daily_cost
attributes.help_text: Average daily cost per person in EUR

attributes.name: total_estimated_cost_7days
attributes.help_text: Total estimated cost for 7 days (excluding flights)

value.name: Sagrada Familia
value.description: Gaudí's unfinished basilica masterpiece, UNESCO site with stunning stained glass and organic architecture

value.name: Park Güell
value.description: Whimsical public park with Gaudí's colorful mosaics, serpentine benches, and city views

value.name: Las Ramblas
value.description: Famous tree-lined pedestrian boulevard with street performers, markets, and outdoor cafés

value.name: Gothic Quarter (Barri Gòtic)
value.description: Medieval neighborhood with narrow streets, Roman ruins, and Barcelona Cathedral

value.name: Casa Batlló
value.description: Gaudí's fantastical modernist building with bone-like balconies and dragon-scale roof

value.name: La Boqueria Market
value.description: Vibrant public market with fresh produce, seafood, jamón, and tapas stalls since 1217

value.name: Barceloneta Beach
value.description: Popular urban beach with golden sand, beach bars, and Mediterranean swimming

value.name: Montjuïc
value.description: Hill with Olympic Stadium, Magic Fountain, museums, and panoramic city views
